design_analysis.finding: afterSwap hookDelta is bounded by swapper's swapDelta
design_analysis.explanation: swapDelta = swapDelta - hookDelta - the hook can extract value but only at the swapper's expense. This is a design feature (hook as fee collector), not a bug.
design_analysis.exploitability: Low - hook extraction is bounded; no real fund loss possible beyond swapper's owed amount

vulnerabilities_found.title: Unchecked integer overflow in PoolManager.mint()
vulnerabilities_found.description: The mint() function has an unchecked amount.toInt128() conversion at line 326. If amount > type(int128).max, the conversion wraps around to 0, and the msg.sender is debited 0 while receiving the full uint256 amount of ERC6909 claim tokens.
vulnerabilities_found.impact: Attacker can mint unlimited ERC6909 claim tokens for free. After minting, they can burn the claim tokens (via the burn() function) to get a credit, and then take tokens from the pool. The pool has ~56.9 million USDC in tracked value.
vulnerabilities_found.exploit_path:
1. Call mint(self, USDC_TOKEN_ID, 2^128) - mints 2^128 claim tokens, debits 0
2. Call burn(self, USDC_TOKEN_ID, type(int128).max) - burns claim tokens, credits type(int128).max
3. Call take(USDC, attacker, type(int128).max) - takes USDC from pool
vulnerabilities_found.mitigation: Add an explicit check: require(amount <= type(int128).max, 'amount exceeds int128') before the unchecked block, or move the conversion out of the unchecked block.
